tiles.question: AVX-SWEEP: eisenstein_snap vectorization study on Ryzen AI 9 HX 370 (24 cores)
tiles.answer:
Key finding: AVX-512 flags alone provide ZERO speedup on Eisenstein snap.
The bottleneck is -ffast-math enabling vectorization of round() + neighbor distance.

Results (1M points, 100 trials):
- Scalar AoS: 14.4 ns/snap (69M/s)
- AVX-512 AoS: 14.3 ns/snap (70M/s) — NO improvement
- AVX-512 SoA: 26.2 ns/snap (38M/s) — SLOWER (strided access without vectorization)
- AVX-512 SoA + -ffast-math: 1.6 ns/snap (621M/s) — 9x speedup!
- AVX-512 SoA + -ffast-math @10M: 1.7 ns/snap (597M/s) — holds at scale

Conclusion: The algorithm needs -ffast-math to vectorize because round() has
strict IEEE semantics. With relaxed math, SoA layout lets GCC auto-vectorize
the inner loop across 8 doubles per AVX-512 register.

Accuracy: max snap distance 0.540 < bound 0.577. Idempotence: 0 failures.
-ffast-math does NOT break correctness for this workload.
